Home → Discography → Still Creepin on Ah Come Up

Still Creepin on Ah Come Up

2008 · Mo Thugs Records

A collaborative album by Bizzy Bone and Layzie Bone released under the Bone Thugs banner, continuing their Bone Brothers partnership under a new name. A nod to the original 1994 classic that launched their career. Featuring both members' signature rapid harmonics across 50 minutes of material. “Conspiracy“ questions the circumstances surrounding Eazy-E's rapid decline and death, but stands as much as a tribute to their late mentor as it does an accusation. The album received positive reviews from die-hard fans.

"Hit 'em with the rhythm like we did it, like back in the day-day."

— Back in the Day
01 Bone Thugs (Intro) produced by Big Hollis ▷
02 Back in the Day produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
03 Make It a Double produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
04 1, 2, 3 produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
05 Shit 2 Do (Skit) produced by Real Talk Entertainment —
06 Let Me Smoke With Ya produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
07 Conspiracy produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
08 Murda (Skit) produced by Real Talk Entertainment —
09 Ready For War produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
10 Rollin, Drinkin produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
11 Prisoner produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
12 It's Still Love produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
13 Give Me Some Hydro produced by Big Hollis & Preach ▷
14 Still Creepin' On Ah Come Up produced by Big Hollis ▷
